_meta.author: Claude Opus 4.8 (va-legislature-refine autopilot)
_meta.note: VA Senate batch SD26-31 (state tier). Evidence-based finalization from VPAP/LIS primary roll calls, sponsorship records, ReproRising 2025/2026 scorecards, Giffords endorsements, Ballotpedia, and news primary reporting. McDougle (R-26) & Reeves (R-28): genuinely conservative HARD records, both confirmed NOT among the GOP same-sex-marriage-amendment crossovers. McPike (D-29) & Roem (D-30): uniformly progressive HARD records. Perry (D-31): progressive on life/marriage/guns/immigration BUT earns documented public-justice credit as a former Loudoun prosecutor (sole Senate Democrat to back the fentanyl felony-homicide bill). Every TRUE/FALSE cites a source; cells with no public evidence left null (not inferred from party).

profile.background: Virginia State Senator, District 26 (Hanover/Caroline area); Senate Republican (Minority) Leader since 2024. In the Senate since 2006 (House of Delegates 2002-2006). Attorney (McDougle Law Firm, Hanover); JMU + William & Mary Law. Committees: Rules; Finance & Appropriations; Courts of Justice; Commerce & Labor; Rehabilitation & Social Services.

ryan-mcdougle.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-26; Senate Republican Leader since 2024. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Genuinely conservative HARD record; cross-party deviation check CLEARED (voted NO on the same-sex-marriage repeal amendment HJ3 2026 and NO on the reproductive-freedom amendment HJ1 2026 per VPAP close-votes). Pro-life (ReproRising F; NO on repro amendment, NO on contraception statute HB6). Marriage: NO on the SSM-repeal constitutional amendment. Election integrity: sponsored photo-voter-ID bills SB764 & SB794, NO on HB26 (expanding non-photo ID). Public justice: chairs Finance public-safety subcommittee, NO on HB45 (earned-sentence-credit expansion) and HB77 (robbery-degree reductions). Immigration: sponsored SB641 (correctional-facility immigration queries) and SB1361 (incarcerated-to-ICE transfer); NO on HB77 2026 (limiting local immigration enforcement). 2A: NO on AWB (HB2 2024), HB46, HB19/HB21/HB40 (2026); VCDL top pro-gun ranking. Federalism / Christian-liberty / family-curriculum cells left null — no isolated cited record found.

0.note: Consistent pro-life record: ReproRising grade F; voted NO on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJ1, 2026) and NO on contraception statute HB6.

4.note: VPAP top-donor base is Republican/business/NRA ($57,500 NRA); no Planned Parenthood/NARAL/EMILY's List/abortion-industry PAC present (top-tier donor review).

0.note: Voted NO on HJ3 (2026), the constitutional amendment to repeal Virginia's same-sex-marriage prohibition — affirms marriage as man+woman; not among the GOP crossovers.

1.note: Opposed codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (NO on HJ3 2026; SJ249 2025 first reference passed with only 3 GOP yes votes, McDougle opposing).
Sponsored voter photo-ID bills SB764 and SB794; voted NO on HB26 (2024) expanding accepted non-photo voter-ID forms.

0.note: Chairs the Finance subcommittee on public safety; campaigns on 'Making Our Neighborhoods Safer,' death penalty and no-parole; affirmative pro-law-enforcement posture.

1.note: Anti-leniency votes: NO on HB45 (2024, expanded earned-sentence credits) and NO on HB77 (2024, robbery-degree reductions).

2.note: Sponsored SB641 (correctional-facility immigration/alien queries) and SB1361 (transfer of incarcerated persons to ICE); voted NO on HB77 (2026) limiting local enforcement of federal immigration matters.

1.note: Opposed gun-control slate: NO on HB2 (2024 assault-firearms ban), HB46 (2024), and HB19/HB21/HB40 (2026); VCDL top pro-gun ranking.

profile.religion: Christian — Baptist; deacon and Bible-study leader at Spotswood Baptist Church, Fredericksburg; Gideons member
profile.background: Virginia State Senator, District 28 (Culpeper/Orange/Spotsylvania area); Senate Republican Whip. In the Senate since 2012 (SD-17, now SD-28). U.S. Army Ranger veteran and former narcotics task-force detective; insurance broker. Ran for Lt. Governor (2017) and U.S. House VA-7 (2022); announced then withdrew a 2026 U.S. Senate bid.

bryce-reeves.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-28; Senate Republican Whip; Army Ranger + former narcotics detective; Baptist deacon. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Strongly conservative HARD record; cross-party deviation check CLEARED (not among the 5 named GOP same-sex-marriage-amendment crossovers; NAY on the SJ247 reproductive-freedom amendment). Pro-life: advanced the 2012 HB1 'personhood' bill; self-described '100% pro-life.' Marriage/gender: sponsored SB1203 (2023) 'Children Deserve Help Not Harm Act' banning minor gender-transition procedures; voted against the LGBT hate-crimes expansion SB112 (2018). Family: sponsored SB1199 (parental educational transparency), SB908 (parental fundamental rights). 2A: NO on SB70 (UBC 2020), SB240 (red-flag 2020), HB1083 (safe-storage 2020); A+ NRA, NRA-PVF endorsed. Public justice: founded Virginia's first Veterans Treatment Court, first-responder caucus co-chair. Election-integrity, immigration, federalism, economic, and granular Christian-liberty cells left null — genuine NO EVIDENCE FOUND after multiple searches (recommend a manual VPAP roll-call pass to upgrade).

0.note: Voted to advance HB1 (2012 'personhood' bill recognizing personhood from conception); self-described '100%, unequivocally pro-life'; NAY on the 2025 reproductive-freedom amendment SJ247.
Opposed the same-sex-marriage repeal amendment — not among the named GOP crossovers (DeSteph, Diggs, Durant, Suetterlein) who backed HJ3/SJ249.

1.note: Voted against codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (not a crossover on the SSM-repeal amendment).

2.note: Sponsored SB1203 (2023), the 'Children Deserve Help Not Harm Act,' banning gender-transition procedures for minors and barring public funds/insurance — affirms biological sex.

4.note: Opposes state promotion of gender ideology (SB1203 bars public funding of minor transition); voted against the LGBT-inclusive hate-crimes bill SB112 (2018).

1.note: Sponsored SB1203 (minor gender-procedure ban), SB1199 (parental educational transparency), and SB908 declaring a parent's fundamental right to direct a child's upbringing/education/care.

0.note: Former narcotics detective who founded Virginia's first Veterans Treatment Court and co-chairs the military/veterans/first-responder caucus — affirmative pro-law-enforcement record.

1.note: Voted against universal background checks (SB70 2020), the red-flag law (SB240 2020), and safe-storage (HB1083 2020); A+ NRA, NRA-PVF endorsed.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND (not stated in Ballotpedia, Wikipedia, VPAP, or VoteSmart)
profile.background: Virginia State Senator, District 29 (Prince William/Manassas area); in the Senate since 2016. Chairs Senate General Laws & Technology. Former 22-year City of Alexandria employee (Director of General Services) and Dale City volunteer fire captain.

jeremy-mcpike.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-29; chairs General Laws & Technology. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record. Life: voted YES on the reproductive-freedom amendment SJ247 (Senate 21-19, individually confirmed via roll call); ReproRising A+ 'Champion.' Marriage: co-sponsor of SJR249 (same-sex-marriage repeal amendment); supported SB1098 gender-affirming-healthcare protections. Immigration: voted NO on SB1156 (2019), the bill to ban localities from adopting sanctuary policies (protected sanctuary discretion). 2A: part of the 2020 majority passing the red-flag law (SB240) and universal background checks (SB70); authored a bill removing the online concealed-carry-course option; Giffords-endorsed 'gun safety champion'; ~$1.5M in Everytown backing (2015). No conservative deviation found with citable proof; family/Christian-liberty/economic/federalism cells left null (no public record located).

0.note: Voted YES on SJ247 (reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment); ReproRising A+ 'Champion' — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted YES on the reproductive-freedom amendment (SJ247, Senate 21-19) — opposite of abortion-abolition.

0.note: Co-sponsor of SJR249 repealing Virginia's same-sex-marriage ban — does not affirm marriage as man+woman.

1.note: Co-sponsored the constitutional amendment codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

2.note: Supported SB1098 protections for gender-affirming healthcare.
Voted NO on SB1156 (2019), which would have banned localities from adopting sanctuary policies — protected sanctuary discretion.

0.note: Authored legislation removing the online concealed-carry-course option (tightening carry); Giffords-endorsed — opposite of permitless constitutional carry.

1.note: Part of the 2020 majority passing the red-flag/ERPO law (SB240) and universal background checks (SB70); Giffords credits his support for both.

profile.religion: Raised Catholic; self-describes as 'non-practicing'/'fallen away' but still identifies Catholic (secondary report)
profile.background: Virginia State Senator, District 30 (Prince William/Manassas/Manassas Park); in the Senate since 2024, previously House of Delegates 2018-2024. Former local journalist. Nationally notable as the first openly transgender person elected and seated in a U.S. state legislature, and the first openly transgender state senator in the U.S. South.

danica-roem.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-30; first openly transgender U.S. state senator. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record and an active leader of the measures most opposed by this rubric. Life: sponsor of the reproductive-freedom amendment SJR247; pledges to protect abortion/IVF access. Marriage: on-record 'long overdue that we repealed Marshall Newman… affirmative right to marry'; sponsor/patron of the SSM-repeal amendment (HJ3, Senate 26-13, Jan 16 2026); championed pro-transgender measures; opposes school parental-notification-on-gender bills; ended anti-trans insurance discrimination (2020). 2A: Giffords-endorsed; on-record 'because of my support, we have a red flag law'; votes for UBC + ERPO. Election/justice/border/federalism/economic cells left null (no public record located on those specific items). VPAP donor itemization (abortion/gun PACs) blocked to automated fetch — endorsements documented, dollar figures not quoted.

0.note: Co-sponsor of SJ247 establishing a fundamental right to reproductive freedom — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Sponsor of the reproductive-freedom amendment headed to the 2026 ballot; pledges to protect access to 'safe, legal abortion' — opposite of abortion-abolition.

0.note: On record: 'long overdue that we repealed Marshall Newman… replaced it with an affirmative right to marry' — rejects marriage as man+woman.

1.note: Sponsor/patron of the same-sex-marriage repeal amendment (HJ3, Senate agreed 26-13, Jan 16 2026).

2.note: First openly transgender legislator; sponsored and championed pro-transgender legislation — does not affirm immutable biological sex.

4.note: Opposes bills requiring schools to notify parents when a child identifies as a different gender; sponsored HB2132; ended anti-trans discrimination in health insurance (2020).

1.note: Opposes legislation requiring schools to notify parents of a child's gender identity — contrary to parental notification/consent on gender interventions.
Giffords credits her votes for universal background checks, local firearm regulation, and the extreme-risk protection order (red-flag) law; on record: 'Because of my support, we have a red flag law.'

profile.religion: Christian (per official Senate member page; denomination not specified)
profile.background: Virginia State Senator, District 31 (Loudoun/Fauquier); in the Senate since 2024. Former CIA officer and former assistant Commonwealth's Attorney (prosecutor) in Loudoun County. George Washington University + Georgetown Law. Committees: Courts of Justice; Education & Health; Agriculture; Privileges & Elections.

russet-perry.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-31; former CIA officer and Loudoun County prosecutor. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Progressive on the social dimensions with one genuine, documented conservative exception on public justice. Life: co-patron of SJR247/HJ1 (reproductive-freedom amendment); ReproRising A+ 'Champion'; opposes abortion bans even with rape/incest exceptions. Marriage: co-patron of SJR249 (same-sex-marriage repeal amendment, Senate 24-15). 2A: Giffords + Moms Demand endorsed; campaigned on magazine limits and an assault-weapons ban ('weapons of war… don't belong in the hands of a civilian'). Immigration: opposed SB1141 (mandatory transfer of incarcerated persons to ICE) — protected local discretion. PUBLIC-JUSTICE CREDIT (earned, cited): as a former prosecutor she was the SOLE Senate Democrat to vote for the fentanyl felony-homicide bill (Courts of Justice, Jan 2024), siding with Republicans and rejecting the public-health-only framing ('those trafficking in fentanyl should be held accountable'). Net documented score reflects real cross-pressure, not a party default. Christian-liberty/economic/federalism/family cells null (no cited record).

0.note: Co-patron of SJR247/HJ1 enshrining a fundamental right to abortion/contraception/IVF; ReproRising A+ 'Champion'; opposes abortion bans even with rape/incest exceptions.

1.note: Sponsored the reproductive-freedom amendment and publicly opposes abortion restrictions — opposite of abortion-abolition.

0.note: Co-patron of SJR249 (2025) repealing Virginia's same-sex-marriage ban and creating an affirmative right to marry (Senate 24-15).

1.note: Co-sponsored the constitutional amendment codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

0.note: Former Loudoun County prosecutor who ran on public safety and crossed her own caucus to back fentanyl-dealer accountability — affirmative pro-law-enforcement record.

1.note: Sole Senate Democrat to vote (with Republicans) for the fentanyl felony-homicide bill in Courts of Justice (Jan 2024); 'those trafficking in fentanyl… should be held accountable.'

3.note: Argued for 'a separate and distinct charge of felony homicide' for fentanyl deaths — rejects the public-health-only framing as an excuse to avoid punishment.

2.note: Opposed SB1141 (mandatory notification/transfer of incarcerated persons to ICE): 'I'm not sure that we need to also take on the responsibilities of the federal government' — protects local sanctuary discretion.

1.note: Giffords + Moms Demand endorsed; campaigned on magazine-capacity limits and an assault-weapons ban; 'weapons of war… don't belong in the hands of a civilian.'
